DISCOVERY 301, ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS -- CROSS COMPLAINANT CHILL-CHAIN, INC.'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S OBLIGATION WILL RESPOND WITH RESPECT TO INDEPENDANT DISTRIBUTERS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO CROSS-COMPLAINANT SEEKING BROADER CLASSIFICATION OF DOCUMENTS, WITHIN 10 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER AND DEFENDANT PROVIDING PLAINTIFF WITH A LIST OF ITS CUSTOMERS. THE PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER TO AGREE ON A PROTECTIVE ORDER. IF THEY HAVE NOT AGREED BY 5/10, EACH PARTY SHALL SUBMIT ITS PROPOSAL TO THE COURT BY 5/11 AND ITS OBJECTIONS TO THE OTHER SIDE'S PROPOSAL BY 5/13. THE COURT WILL THEN ENTER THE ORDER IT DEEMS MOST REASONABLE. PLAINTIFF MUST ALSO PRODUCE A PRIVILEGE LOG BY 5/11 FOR ALL DOCUMENTS IT WITHHELD ON A CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE. PLAINTIFF MUST PAY SANCTIONS TO DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,180.00. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, NOT REPORTED (301/PJB)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT BERKELEY FARMS LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON CLAIMS FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, OPEN BOOK ACCOUNT AND GOODS SOLD AND DELIVERED. A R G U E D; THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED AS TO THE COMPLAINT, MET ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION AND NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANTS BERKELEY FARMS LLC AND CROSS-DEFENDANT DEAN FOODS COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO CHILL CHAIN'S CROSS-COMPLAINT. A R G U E D; THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO ALL SIX CLAIMS IN THE CCI CROSS-COMPLAINT ON THE GROUNDS THAT BERKELEY FARMS AND DEAN FOODS HAVE MET ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION AND THERE HAS BEEN NO EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE. HOWEVER, INSTEAD OF GRANTING SUMMARY JUDGMENT, THE COURT GRANTED LEAVE FOR CCI TO ALLEGE A NEW AND DIFFERENT CLAIM FOR INTERFERENCE WITH ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE AS TO ITS CUSTOMERS, BUT NOT AS TO SANTA CLARA DAIRY. THE NEW CLAIM TO BE FILED WITHIN TEN DAYS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G).) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT BERKELEY FARMS LLC AND CROSS-DEFENDANT DEAN FOODS COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is Sustained with ten days leave to amend. CCI is given leave to amend, if it can do so in good faith, to allege the person(s) who made the alleged misrepresentations, to whom they were made, when they were made, and the manner in which they were communicated. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 = (302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-22-2012 Continued To Mar-13-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ADAM FRIEDENBERG OF GLYNN & FINLEY LLP, TEL. NO. (925) 210-2809 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SHAWN M. KROGH OF RADOSLOVICH/KROGH, TEL. NO. (916) 565-8161 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANTS BERKELEY FARMS LLC AND CROSS-DEFENDANT DEAN FOODS COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO CHILL CHAIN'S CROSS-COMPLAINT - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO ALL SIX CLAIMS IN THE CCI CROSS-COMPLAINT ON THE GROUNDS THAT BERKELEY FARMS AND DEAN FOODS HAVE MET ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION AND THERE HAS BEEN NO EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE. HOWEVER, INSTEAD OF GRANTING SUMMARY JUDGMENT, THE COURT GRANTED LEAVE FOR CCI TO ALLEGE A NEW AND DIFFERENT CLAIM FOR INTERFERENCE WITH ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE AS TO ITS CUSTOMERS, BUT NOT AS TO SANTA CLARA DAIRY. THE NEW CLAIM TO BE FILED WITHIN TEN DAYS. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(G).) Judge: Harold Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR #7077 =302/HEK

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions;
Off Calendar May-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Michael Martucci, Esq of Radoslovich & Krogh, 916-565-8170 for defendant/cross-complainant and moving party Chill-Chain, Inc.
Adam Friedenberg, Esq. and Maureen Rodgers, Esq. of Glynn & Finley, 925-210-2809 for plaintiff/cross-defendant and opposing party Berkeley Farms

RULING:
ARGUED and the Court modified its tentative ruling as follows --
CROSS COMPLAINANT CHILL-CHAIN, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions.
Granted. PLAINTIFF'S OBLIGATION WILL RESPOND WITH RESPECT TO INDEPENDANT DISTRIBUTERS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO CROSS-COMPLAINANT SEEKING BROADER CLASSIFICATION OF DOCUMENTS, within 10 days of notice of this order and Defendant providing Plaintiff with a list of its customers. The parties shall meet and confer to agree on a protective order. If they have not agreed by 5/10, each party shall submit its proposal to the court by 5/11 and its objections to the other side's proposal by 5/13. The court will then enter the order it deems most reasonable. Plaintiff must also produce a privilege log by 5/11 for all documents it withheld on a claim of privilege. Plaintiff must pay sanctions to Defendant in the amount of $1,180.00. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Not Reported (301/PJB)
